description: §4.14's own invalid example verbatim — an engine state-child `:`-shorthand body holding two semicolon-separated expressions (`<Idle : startGame(); track("start")>`) fires E-MULTI-STATEMENT-HANDLER. This pins the SECOND fire site (the `:`-shorthand body validator), distinct from the event-handler-attribute site pinned by `multi-stmt-handler-attr-pos`.
rationale: §4.14 ("What is NOT legal under `:`-shorthand"): "Multi-statement: `<Idle : startGame(); track()>` — `E-MULTI-STATEMENT-HANDLER`. Use bare-body." §5.2.3 states the shared discipline: "The same single-expression discipline that governs `:`-shorthand bodies (§4.14) governs bare-form event handlers." The §34 row for the code names BOTH loci ("§5.2.3, §4.14 — a bare-form event-handler attribute value (or a `:`-shorthand body) contains multiple statements"). The post-`:` body here carries a top-level `;`, so the code SHALL fire.
